title: 155 Homes Were Gone by Morning
script: 155 homes, gone overnight? The Aspen Acres Fire tore through southern Colorado's Beulah and Rye on Monday, and 100 mile-per-hour winds turned it into something else entirely — an 8-mile run in a single day. By morning, more than 155 homes and structures were gone across Pueblo and Custer counties. The historic Horseshoe Lodge, standing in Pueblo Mountain Park since the early 1900s, took heavy damage too. Residents had minutes, not hours, to grab what they could and evacuate as the fire jumped ridgelines faster than crews could track it. Colorado's governor has already declared a disaster. Families who've lived in these mountains for generations are now sorting through what didn't burn. So how do you rebuild a town the wind erased in a day?
